children.heading: Agricultural Act of 1949
children.label: 1 Agricultural Act of 1949
children.text: The term “Agricultural Act of 1949” means the Agricultural Act of 1949 ( et seq.), as in effect prior to the suspensions under .7 U.S.C. 1421section 7301 of this title

children.heading: Base acres
children.label: 2 Base acres
children.text: The term “base acres”, with respect to a covered commodity on a farm, means the number of acres established under with respect to the covered commodity on the election made by the owner of the farm under subsection (a) of such section.section 7911 of this title

children.heading: Counter-cyclical payment
children.label: 3 Counter-cyclical payment
children.text: The term “counter-cyclical payment” means a payment made to producers on a farm under .section 7914 of this title

children.heading: Covered commodity
children.label: 4 Covered commodity
children.text: The term “covered commodity” means wheat, corn, grain sorghum, barley, oats, upland cotton, rice, soybeans, and other oilseeds.

children.heading: Direct payment
children.label: 5 Direct payment
children.text: The term “direct payment” means a payment made to producers on a farm under .section 7913 of this title

children.heading: Effective price
children.label: 6 Effective price
children.text: The term “effective price”, with respect to a covered commodity for a crop year, means the price calculated by the Secretary under to determine whether counter-cyclical payments are required to be made for that crop year.section 7914 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is produced from pure strain varieties of the Barbadense species or any hybrid thereof, or other similar types of extra long staple cotton, designated by the Secretary, having characteristics needed for various end uses for which United States upland cotton is not suitable and grown in irrigated cotton-growing regions of the United States designated by the Secretary or other areas designated by the Secretary as suitable for the production of the varieties or types; and

children.label: B
children.text: is ginned on a roller-type gin or, if authorized by the Secretary, ginned on another type gin for experimental purposes.

children.heading: Loan commodity
children.label: 8 Loan commodity
children.text: The term “loan commodity” means wheat, corn, grain sorghum, barley, oats, upland cotton, extra long staple cotton, rice, soybeans, other oilseeds, wool, mohair, honey, dry peas, lentils, and small chickpeas.

children.heading: Other oilseed
children.label: 9 Other oilseed
children.text: The term “other oilseed” means a crop of sunflower seed, rapeseed, canola, safflower, flaxseed, mustard seed, crambe, sesame seed, or, if designated by the Secretary, another oilseed.

children.heading: Payment acres
children.label: 10 Payment acres
children.text: The term “payment acres” means 85 percent of the base acres of a covered commodity on a farm, as established under , on which direct payments and counter-cyclical payments are made.section 7911 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “payment yield” means the yield established under for a farm for a covered commodity.section 7912 of this title

children.heading: Updated payment yield
children.label: B Updated payment yield
children.text: The term “updated payment yield” means the payment yield elected by the owner of a farm under to be used in calculating the counter-cyclical payments for the farm.section 7912(e) of this title

children.heading: Producer
children.label: 12 Producer
children.text: The term “producer” means an owner, operator, landlord, tenant, or sharecropper that shares in the risk of producing a crop and is entitled to share in the crop available for marketing from the farm, or would have shared had the crop been produced. In determining whether a grower of hybrid seed is a producer, the Secretary shall not take into consideration the existence of a hybrid seed contract and shall ensure that program requirements do not adversely affect the ability of the grower to receive a payment under this chapter.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 13 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Target price
children.label: 15 Target price
children.text: The term “target price” means the price per bushel (or other appropriate unit in the case of upland cotton, rice, and other oilseeds) of a covered commodity used to determine the payment rate for counter-cyclical payments.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 16 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.
